overview.summary: The Fast and Slow Pointers pattern (also known as Floyd's Cycle Detection Algorithm) uses two pointers moving at different speeds to detect cycles or find specific elements in a cyclic data structure.
learningObjectives: Find middle of linked list in one pass
overview.interviewRelevance: Crucial for linked list questions involving cycles or finding the middle node without knowing the length.

content.content: By moving one pointer at twice the speed of another, they are mathematically guaranteed to meet if a cycle exists. If there is no cycle, the fast pointer will simply reach the end.

codeHtml.python:
def has_cycle(head):
  slow, fast = head, head
  while fast and fast.next:
    slow = slow.next
    fast = fast.next.next
    if slow == fast:
      return True
  return False
codeHtml.javascript:
function hasCycle(head) {
  let slow = head, fast = head;
  while (fast && fast.next) {
    slow = slow.next;
    fast = fast.next.next;
    if (slow === fast) return true;
  }
  return false;
}

steps.explanation: Initialize slow and fast pointers at the head of the linked list.
Move slow by 1, fast by 2. Compare if they meet.

patternTemplate.whenToUse: Dealing with Linked Lists or arrays where indices point to other indices, and you need to detect a cycle or find the middle.
patternTemplate.timeComplexity: O(N) - fast pointer traverses list at most twice.
patternTemplate.spaceComplexity: O(1) - only two pointers used regardless of list size.

examples.title: Linked List Cycle
examples.problemStatement: Given head, the head of a linked list, determine if the linked list has a cycle in it.
steps: Initialize slow and fast at head

recognitionQuiz.problemStatement: Find the middle node of a Linked List in a single pass without storing nodes in an array.
recognitionQuiz.explanation: A slow pointer moving at 1x speed will exactly reach the middle when a fast pointer moving at 2x speed reaches the end.

conceptQuiz.question: Why does the fast pointer move exactly twice as fast?
options: It guarantees they will meet in O(N) time without skipping each other.
It is required for O(1) space.
It is the only speed that works.
conceptQuiz.explanation: If fast moved 3x as fast, it could potentially 'skip' the slow pointer in certain cycle lengths.
